q: What is the FIRST step to create a code component from the CLI?
o: Install the Power Platform CLI (and Node.js)
why: You install the CLI (and Node.js) before scaffolding a PCF project.

q: Which command scaffolds a new PCF component project?
why: pac pcf init creates the component project structure.

q: Decide whether each project-setup statement is true.

rows.s: npm install restores the project's dependencies.
The manifest is edited to declare properties before coding logic.
PCF projects require no package manager.

why: npm restores dependencies; you edit the manifest; PCF relies on npm.

q: After adding a new property to the component, what must you do so the typings update?
o: Rebuild (npm build) to regenerate the manifest typings
why: Rebuilding regenerates the strongly typed ManifestTypes used in code.

q: Where do you write the component's rendering/behavior logic?
why: index.ts implements the lifecycle methods (init/updateView/getOutputs/destroy).

q: How do you add CSS styling to a code component?
o: Add a CSS resource and reference it in the manifest
why: Styles are added as CSS resources referenced from the manifest.

q: Decide whether each logic/styling statement is true.

rows.s: updateView is where you read context and render output.
getOutputs returns values back to the app/framework.
destroy is called when the component initializes.

why: updateView renders, getOutputs returns values, destroy runs on teardown (not init).

q: Which command compiles the component during development?
why: npm run build (or npm start for the harness) compiles the TypeScript/resources.

q: Which tool lets you run and debug the component locally without deploying?
o: The Power Apps component test harness (npm start)
why: npm start launches the local test harness to run/debug the component.

q: To deploy the component to a Dataverse environment during development, you...
why: pac pcf push deploys the built component to the environment for testing.

q: Decide whether each build/test statement is true.

rows.s: The test harness simulates context inputs and data inputs.
You can iterate: change code, rebuild, and re-run the harness.

why: The harness feeds context/data inputs and supports rapid rebuild-run cycles.

q: For proper ALM, how should a finished component be delivered?
why: Components ship inside solutions for versioning and deployment (ALM).

q: Which command creates a solution project to package the component?
why: pac solution init creates a solution project; you then add the component reference.

q: After pac solution init, how do you include the component in the solution?
o: pac solution add-reference to the component project
Add it to the app title
why: pac solution add-reference links the component project into the solution.

q: Decide whether each packaging statement is true.

rows.s: Building the solution project produces an importable solution.
The solution can be built managed for deployment.
A component cannot be part of a solution.

why: Building the solution project yields a solution (managed/unmanaged) containing the component.

q: Context inputs vs data inputs in the harness differ how?
o: Context inputs simulate the runtime context; data inputs supply the bound data values
why: The harness separates simulated context from the actual bound data values you test with.

q: A component renders once but never updates when the field changes. Which method likely isn't handling changes?
why: updateView must react to context/data changes to re-render on updates.

q: Decide whether each end-to-end statement is true.

rows.s: The typical flow is init -> build -> test harness -> push -> package.
npm build regenerates manifest typings after property changes.

why: Scaffold, build, test, push and package is the standard PCF flow; rebuild refreshes typings.

q: Which command builds the solution project into a deployable solution file?
o: dotnet build (or msbuild) on the solution project
why: Building the .cdsproj solution project (dotnet build/msbuild) produces the importable solution .zip.

q: Decide whether each solution-packaging statement is true for a code component.

rows.s: The built solution can be imported into another environment.
You can produce a managed build for deployment.
A code component cannot be shipped in a solution.

why: The solution build yields an importable artifact (managed or unmanaged) that carries the component.
